questions.scenario: `orders.devpolaris.com` should point at `fd-devpolaris-orders.azurefd.net`. Which record type is a natural fit?
questions.explanation: A CNAME maps one name to another name. TXT is for text values such as ownership proof.

questions.scenario: What does a lower TTL help with during a planned cutover?

options.text: Resolvers may refresh the answer sooner after the record changes.
The TLS certificate becomes trusted automatically.
The SQL private endpoint changes IP address.

questions.explanation: TTL affects how long DNS answers can be cached. It does not fix TLS, backend health, or private endpoint state.

questions.scenario: Which mistakes can break a custom-domain cutover?

options.text: CNAME points at the old entry point.
TXT ownership proof is missing or wrong.

questions.explanation: The public name, ownership proof, and certificate all matter. Managed identity is important, but it is not what breaks this DNS/TLS cutover.
